Class: RoutingRoute

bemap.RoutingRoute()

new RoutingRoute()

BeNomad BeMap JavaScript API - RoutingRoute A single calculated route. A response can contain several when the request asked for alternatives or used MODE_1_TO_N / N_TO_1 / N_TO_N. Wire shape from the BeMap server (cross-checked against an actual response payload): { length: long, // total length in metres duration: long, // total duration in seconds (no traffic) totalDuration: long, // total duration in seconds (with traffic, if requested) trafficDelay: long, departureTime: long, // epoch ms (0 when not set) arrivalTime: long, // epoch ms (0 when not set) averageSpeed: double, // km/h maximumSpeed: double, startUTurnThreshold: int, routingInstructions: [...], // turn-by-turn boundingBox: { minLon, minLat, maxLon, maxLat }, startStopInfo: { start, stop, distanceFirstMatched, distanceLastMatched, interDests }, polyline: [{ lon, lat }, ...] // array of coordinate objects } Extras returned by the server but not modelled here are preserved on `extra` so advanced consumers can still reach them. For consumer convenience, `getSummary()` returns a synthetic `bemap.RoutingSummary` built from the route-level totals (distance, duration, traffic delay, ETA).
Since:
  • 1.5.0
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/routing/bemap-routing-route.js, line 36

getMaximumSpeed()

Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/routing/bemap-routing-route.js, line 74

getPolyline() → {Array.<bemap.Coordinate>|null}

Returns the polyline as an Array of `bemap.Coordinate`. The BeMap server emits the polyline as an array of `{lon, lat}` objects when the POLYLINE option is requested; we coerce to `bemap.Coordinate` for ergonomics. Returns `null` when the polyline was not requested.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/routing/bemap-routing-route.js, line 86
Returns:
Type
Array.<bemap.Coordinate> | null

getPolylineRaw()

Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/routing/bemap-routing-route.js, line 104

getSummary() → {bemap.RoutingSummary}

Synthetic summary object: distance, duration, traffic delay, ETA. Built from the route-level totals — preserved for parity with consumers that expect a `summary` object on the route.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/routing/bemap-routing-route.js, line 122
Returns:
Type
bemap.RoutingSummary
